title: The man who waters the plants at night
summary: Describe a worker most people never see, and explain why their quiet job matters to a place.
topic: A worker who does a quiet job at night
prompt: Write 8–10 sentences describing someone who does invisible work at night, such as cleaning, watering plants, or fixing things. Describe what they do, when they do it, and why most people never notice. Use present simple, past simple, and the connector 'because'.
hintStarters: Most people in my office have never met the man who…
He comes in after everyone has gone home, and…
I only noticed him because one evening I stayed late and…
His job seems small, but without him the plants would…

hintVocab.meaningVi: bị bỏ qua, không được để ý

sampleText: There are about forty plants in my office in Manila, and most people think they take care of themselves. They do not. A man named Ernesto waters them every night, but almost nobody has met him. He arrives at nine, after the last meeting ends and the lights in most rooms are off. He carries a green watering can and moves from desk to desk in the dark, touching the soil with one finger before he pours. I only noticed him because one evening I stayed late to finish a report. At first I was a little surprised, because I had worked there for two years and never thought about who kept the plants alive. We talked for a few minutes. He told me he had thirty plants at home too, and that some of ours were thirsty and some were not, and you had to learn the difference. He said the big plant near the window always needed more water because the sun dried it out by noon. The next morning, the office looked the same as always: green and ordinary. Nobody said anything about the plants, because nobody knew they had been watered. I think that is the strange thing about his job. When he does it well, it goes unnoticed. Now I look at the leaves and remember that someone came in the dark to keep them green.

sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: gần như không ai gặp anh ấy

sampleAnnotations.phrase: the lights in most rooms are off
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: đèn ở hầu hết các phòng đã tắt

sampleAnnotations.phrase: touching the soil with one finger
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: chạm vào đất bằng một ngón tay

sampleAnnotations.phrase: I stayed late to finish a report
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: tôi ở lại muộn để hoàn thành báo cáo

sampleAnnotations.phrase: never thought about who kept the plants alive
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: chưa bao giờ nghĩ về ai giữ cho cây sống

sampleAnnotations.phrase: you had to learn the difference
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: bạn phải học cách phân biệt

sampleAnnotations.phrase: the sun dried it out by noon
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: mặt trời làm khô nó vào buổi trưa

sampleAnnotations.phrase: nobody knew they had been watered
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: không ai biết chúng đã được tưới

sampleAnnotations.phrase: When he does it well, it goes unnoticed
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: Khi anh ấy làm tốt, không ai để ý
ai đó đã đến trong bóng tối

sampleGrammarNotes.title: 'Because' to give a reason
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: Câu 'I only noticed him because one evening I stayed late' và 'the big plant near the window always needed more water because the sun dried it out' dùng 'because' để nối một kết quả với lý do của nó. Mệnh đề sau 'because' giải thích tại sao điều trước đó xảy ra.
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: The sentences 'I only noticed him because one evening I stayed late' and 'the big plant near the window always needed more water because the sun dried it out' use 'because' to link a result to its reason. The clause after 'because' explains why the first thing happened.

sampleTranslationVi: Có khoảng bốn mươi cây trong văn phòng của tôi ở Manila, và hầu hết mọi người nghĩ chúng tự chăm sóc bản thân. Không phải vậy. Một người đàn ông tên Ernesto tưới chúng mỗi đêm, nhưng gần như không ai gặp anh ấy. Anh đến lúc chín giờ, sau khi cuộc họp cuối cùng kết thúc và đèn ở hầu hết các phòng đã tắt. Anh cầm một bình tưới màu xanh và đi từ bàn này sang bàn khác trong bóng tối, chạm vào đất bằng một ngón tay trước khi tưới. Tôi chỉ để ý đến anh vì một buổi tối tôi ở lại muộn để hoàn thành báo cáo. Lúc đầu tôi hơi ngạc nhiên, vì tôi đã làm việc ở đó hai năm mà chưa bao giờ nghĩ về ai giữ cho cây sống. Chúng tôi nói chuyện vài phút. Anh kể anh cũng có ba mươi cây ở nhà, và rằng một số cây của chúng tôi khát nước còn một số thì không, và bạn phải học cách phân biệt. Anh nói cây lớn gần cửa sổ luôn cần nhiều nước hơn vì mặt trời làm khô nó vào buổi trưa. Sáng hôm sau, văn phòng trông giống như mọi khi: xanh và bình thường. Không ai nói gì về cây cối, vì không ai biết chúng đã được tưới. Tôi nghĩ đó là điều kỳ lạ về công việc của anh. Khi anh làm tốt, không ai để ý. Bây giờ tôi nhìn những chiếc lá và nhớ rằng ai đó đã đến trong bóng tối để giữ cho chúng xanh tươi.

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the best-written sentence describing a worker who comes after others leave.
options: He arrives after everyone has went home.
He arrives later than everybody is leaving the home.
He arrives after everyone has gone home.
He arrives when the meeting which the staff attend it ends.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Has gone home' is the correct present perfect. 'Has went' uses the wrong participle; the third option is awkward and unnatural; the fourth adds a wrong extra pronoun 'it'.
mcQuestions.hint: What is the correct past participle of 'go'?

mcQuestions.prompt: Pick the most natural sentence about a job nobody sees.
options: When he does the job good, it goes unnoticed.
When he makes the job well, it goes unnoticed.
When he does well the job, it is gone unnoticed.
When he does the job well, it goes unnoticed.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Does the job well' uses the correct adverb and verb. 'Good' is an adjective, not an adverb; 'makes the job' is the wrong verb; 'is gone unnoticed' misuses the passive.
mcQuestions.hint: Do you 'do' or 'make' a job? Is 'good' an adjective or adverb?

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the correct sentence using 'because' to give a reason.
options: The plant needed more water because the sun dried it out.
The plant needed more water because of the sun dried it out.
The plant needed more water, because the sun it dried out.
The plant needed more water so the sun dried it out.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Because + clause' correctly gives the reason. 'Because of' needs a noun, not a clause; the third reorders words wrongly; 'so' shows result, not reason.
mcQuestions.hint: Does 'because' or 'because of' come before a full clause?

mcQuestions.prompt: Select the sentence that best says the office looked normal the next day.
options: The next morning, the office looked same as always.
The next morning, the office looked the same as always.
The next morning, the office looked like the same than always.
The next morning, the office was looking the same like always.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'The same as always' is the correct fixed phrase. Option 2 drops 'the'; option 3 mixes 'like' and 'than' wrongly; option 4 misuses the continuous for a state.

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the most natural way to say he checks the soil first.
options: He touches the soil by one finger before he pours.
He touches the soil with a one finger before to pour.
He touches the soil with one finger before he pours.
He is touching the soil with one finger before he will pour.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'With one finger' and 'before he pours' are correct. 'By one finger' is wrong; 'a one finger' adds an extra article; the last uses awkward future and continuous forms.
mcQuestions.hint: Do we say 'with' or 'by' for the body part used?

mcQuestions.prompt: Pick the sentence that best expresses he had never thought about something.
options: I had never thought about who keeps the plants alive then.
I never had think about who kept the plants alive.
I had never think about who kept the plants alive.
I had never thought about who kept the plants alive.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Had never thought' is the correct past perfect. Option 2 mixes tenses oddly; options 3 and 4 use the base verb 'think' instead of the participle 'thought'.
mcQuestions.hint: After 'had', which form of the verb do you need?

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the best sentence about staying late at work.
options: I stayed late to finish a report.
I stayed lately to finish a report.
I stayed late for finish a report.
I was stayed late to finish a report.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Stayed late' uses the adverb 'late' correctly; 'to finish' shows purpose. 'Lately' means 'recently'; 'for finish' is wrong; 'was stayed' is an incorrect passive.
mcQuestions.hint: Is 'lately' the same as 'late'? Which means 'recently'?

mcQuestions.prompt: Select the most natural sentence about learning to tell two things apart.
options: You have to learn the different.
You have to learn the difference.
You have to learn the differ.
You have to learning the difference.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'The difference' is the correct noun. 'Different' is an adjective; 'differ' is a verb; 'have to learning' uses the wrong verb form.
mcQuestions.hint: Which is the noun: difference, different, or differ?

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the sentence that best describes the man moving between desks.
options: He moves from desk to the desk in the dark.
He moves desk to desk on the dark.
He moves from desk to desk in the dark.
He moves from a desk until a desk in the dark.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'From desk to desk' is the correct fixed pattern with no articles. The others add wrong articles, use 'on' instead of 'in the dark', or use 'until' for place.
mcQuestions.hint: In the pattern 'from ___ to ___' for repeated movement, do we add 'the'?

mcQuestions.prompt: Pick the best closing sentence about remembering the worker.
options: Now I look the leaves and remember that someone came in the dark.
Now I look at the leaves and remembering someone came in the dark.
Now I am looking the leaves and remember someone come in the dark.
Now I look at the leaves and remember that someone came in the dark.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Look at' needs the preposition 'at', and 'remember that' introduces the clause correctly. The others drop 'at', use the wrong verb form, or mix incorrect tenses.
mcQuestions.hint: Which preposition follows 'look' when you look at something?

criticalThinkingQuestion: Whose quiet work do you depend on every day without ever seeing it? Why do we so easily forget the people who keep ordinary things working?
